docs.title: Enterprise CRM Portal
docs.text:
Customer, order, campaign, and ticketing back-office for the Octo Demo. Also the sole controller of chaos scenarios for the Shop — the Shop has no write surface by design.

See Architecture and the Observability v2 section.

docs.title: Architecture
docs.text: FastAPI + Oracle ATP + OCI APM + OCI Logging + OCI Log Analytics, sharing the same observability contract as the Shop.

docs.title: Cross-service integration
docs.text:
CRM pairs with the OCTO Drone Shop over HTTPS. The wire protocol is pinned on both sides and published at /api/integrations/schema.

  • Canonical env vars: SERVICE_SHOP_URL, INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEY. Legacy aliases (OCTO_DRONE_SHOP_URL, MUSHOP_CLOUDNATIVE_URL, DRONE_SHOP_INTERNAL_KEY) remain accepted and surface a startup deprecation warning.
  • POST /api/orders requires X-Internal-Service-Key whenever the shared key is configured; anonymous traffic is allowed only when the key is empty (back-compat).
  • source_system, source_order_id, and idempotency_token from the payload are stored verbatim so shop-side retries with the same stable UUID5 token deduplicate to the same CRM order.

See integrations/cross-service-contract.md.

docs.title: Correlation contract
docs.text: key origin who joins on it trace_id / oracleApmTraceId OTel APM, Log Analytics, Coordinator request_id (X-Request-Id) middleware WAF ↔ app workflow_id middleware LA dashboards, playbooks

docs.title: Chaos admin
docs.text:
• UI: /admin/chaos (role chaos-operator).
  • API: POST /api/admin/chaos/apply, POST /api/admin/chaos/clear.
  • State: chaos_state table; Shop polls it every CHAOS_STATE_POLL_SECONDS.
  • Audit: every apply/clear lands in the octo-chaos-audit LA parser.

docs.title: Security
docs.text:
• SecurityHeadersMiddleware with CSP nonce; admin pages embeddable only from the Ops portal (allow_framing_from=https://$OPS_DOMAIN).
  • IDCS OIDC roles; rate limits on admin + login.
  • WAF in DETECTION mode (WAF_MODE=DETECTION).

docs.title: Cross-service integration contract
docs.text: Enterprise CRM Portal and OCTO Drone Shop share a defined wire protocol so either side can evolve without breaking the other. This page is the authoritative reference — the same OpenAPI subset is served at /api/integrations/schema on both services.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All cross-service calls carry the shared X-Internal-Service-Key header:

POST /api/orders HTTP/1.1
Host: crm.<tenancy>
Content-Type: application/json
X-Internal-Service-Key: <shared-secret>
  • Canonical env var: INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEY (both sides).
  • Legacy alias still accepted: DRONE_SHOP_INTERNAL_KEY (emits a deprecation warning at startup).
  • When the key is set, POST /api/orders rejects unauthenticated callers with 401. When the key is empty the endpoint accepts anonymous traffic — this is the back-compat default.

docs.title: Idempotency contract
docs.text:
The calling service (Drone Shop) supplies the three dedup fields on every order sync:

{
  "customer_id": 42,
  "items": [{"product_id": 7, "quantity": 1, "unit_price": 49.99}],
  "source_system": "octo-drone-shop",
  "source_order_id": "100",
  "idempotency_token": "5e1a6db6-8c0e-5f1c-9c9a-3b0c2a1f0f01"
}
  • source_system — logical name of the upstream service.
  • source_order_id — stable id from the upstream system (retries use the same value).
  • idempotency_token — stable UUID computed on the shop side from uuid5(namespace, "<order_id>:<source>"). Shop side freezes the namespace UUID forever to guarantee retries regenerate the same token.

CRM honors these fields verbatim instead of generating a new source_order_id. A unique composite DB index on (source_system, source_order_id) (or idempotency_token) is the recommended way to enforce dedup at the persistence layer.

docs.title: Env var harmonization
docs.text:
Concern Canonical Legacy aliases Shop URL (CRM → Shop) SERVICE_SHOP_URL OCTO_DRONE_SHOP_URL, MUSHOP_CLOUDNATIVE_URL CRM URL (Shop → CRM) SERVICE_CRM_URL ENTERPRISE_CRM_URL Shared key INTERNAL_SERVICE_KEY DRONE_SHOP_INTERNAL_KEY (CRM side only)

Either side emits a startup deprecation warning when only a legacy name is set.

docs.title: Discovery
docs.text:
curl -s https://crm.<tenancy>/api/integrations/schema | jq
curl -s https://shop.<tenancy>/api/integrations/schema | jq

Both endpoints advertise the InternalServiceKey security scheme and the order payload shape. Multi-tenancy tooling validates the contract with a simple jq check before traffic flows.

docs.title: Observability v2
docs.text: End-to-end correlation of Shop, CRM, DB, WAF, APM, and Log Analytics, feeding the OCI Coordinator for incident-driven, approval-gated auto-remediation.

docs.title: Pages
docs.text:
• Golden workflows
  • Chaos playbook (CRM-only)
  • Security + WAF observability

docs.title: Shop-side observability (cross-reference)
docs.text:
Log Analytics dashboards, APM drill-down, auto-remediation flow, and the end-to-end demo script live on the shop's docs site since the shop owns the ingestion pipeline:

  • Log Analytics dashboards
  • APM drill-down
  • Auto-remediation flow
  • Demo script

docs.title: One-paragraph overview
docs.text: Every request is tagged with trace_id, request_id, and a logical workflow_id. All logs (app, DB audit, WAF, chaos-audit) flow through a parser that emits the same field names in Log Analytics. Saved searches fan out and re-join on those keys. The Coordinator reads them via dedicated MCP tools, proposes remediation playbooks, and — only for tier-low actions — executes them automatically.

docs.title: Chaos playbook
docs.text:
Shop has no chaos write endpoints

The Octo Drone Shop is a reader only. Chaos scenarios can be applied from either the Enterprise CRM admin page (https://crm.example.cloud/admin/chaos) or from the optional ops portal. Any POST to /api/admin/chaos/* on the shop is a 404.

docs.title: Presets
docs.text: id targets faults default TTL db-slow-checkout shop db.slow, workflow:checkout 300 s crm-sync-fail shop + crm http.502, path:/api/crm/sync 300 s payment-timeout shop http.timeout, path:/api/payments 300 s deadlock-cart shop db.deadlock, workflow:add-to-cart 300 s pool-exhaustion shop + crm db.pool_hold 600 s crm-admin-abuse crm http.burst, path:/api/admin 300 s

docs.title: Apply (CRM operator only)
docs.text:
curl -sS https://crm.example.cloud/api/admin/chaos/apply \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -b session.cookie \
  -d '{"scenario_id":"db-slow-checkout","target":"shop","ttl_seconds":300,"note":"demo"}'

docs.title: Clear
docs.text:
curl -sS -X POST https://crm.example.cloud/api/admin/chaos/clear -b session.cookie

The chaos-cleanup Coordinator playbook also auto-clears stale scenarios (tier low, no approval needed) when they exceed their TTL.

docs.title: Audit trail
docs.text: Every apply/clear is written to the chaos-audit logger. The octo-chaos-audit Log Analytics parser surfaces these rows in the Security Posture dashboard.

docs.title: Security hardening
docs.text:
• Security headers middleware — HSTS, CSP with per-request nonce, X-Frame-Options allowing iframe embedding only from OPS_DOMAIN.
  • Request-id middleware — X-Request-Id generated if absent and echoed on the response, consumed by the WAF parser for log correlation.
  • Role-gated admin surfaces via server/security/auth_deps.py. chaos-operator is enforced on /admin/chaos and /api/admin/chaos/*.
  • OIDC session cookies: Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite=Lax.
  • CI gates: .github/workflows/security-gates.yml (bandit, pip-audit, ruff S-rules, semgrep OWASP, gitleaks, tflint, trivy).

docs.title: Policy
docs.text:
One oci_waf_web_app_firewall_policy per frontend via deploy/terraform/modules/waf/. Rules:

  • OWASP Core Rule Set (managed) → LOG in DETECTION.
  • Admin-path CIDR guard (/api/admin/*) → LOG when request originates outside admin_allow_cidrs.
  • Login burst (/login, /api/auth/login) → LOG after 10 req/min.

Flip var.waf_mode = "BLOCK" once the saved searches show zero false positives for a week. The Coordinator waf-tighten-suggest playbook surfaces a Terraform diff proposal before any apply.

docs.title: Log routing
docs.text:
WAF policy ─► OCI Logging group (var.waf_log_group_id)
                   │
                   └─► Service Connector ─► Log Analytics
                                             parser: octo-waf

Configured via deploy/terraform/modules/log_pipeline/ and root variables waf_log_id_{shop,crm,ops,coordinator}.

docs.title: Correlation
docs.text: X-Request-Id is generated by server/security/request_id.py and echoed back on every response. The WAF parser extracts the same header, so dashboards can join WAF events to app logs by that id alone (no trace-context propagation required at the WAF layer).

docs.title: Golden workflows
docs.text:
Six logical flows that every dashboard, alarm, and playbook groups on.

workflow_id Purpose Typical steps browse-catalog Public storefront browse /shop, /api/products add-to-cart Session write path /api/cart checkout Cross-service, DB-heavy Shop → CRM customer sync → orders → payments → shipments order-history Reads across Shop + CRM /api/orders/history crm-lead-capture CRM-only, DB heavy CRM leads + customers admin-analytics Slow aggregates /api/analytics, /api/campaigns

docs.title: Mapping
docs.text:
Implemented in server/observability/workflow_context.py. Each rule is a regex → (workflow_id, step). OTel spans get workflow.id + workflow.step attributes, and logs are enriched via log_enricher.WorkflowLogEnricher.

Extending: add a tuple to DEFAULT_RULES or pass extra_rules to the middleware. Never rename an existing id — Log Analytics searches, alarms, and Coordinator playbooks reference it as a stable key.

docs.title: Chaos admin operations
docs.text: See the shared chaos playbook. This page captures CRM-specific operational guidance.

docs.title: Access
docs.text: Users must hold the chaos-operator IDCS role. Assign via the IDCS admin console; no code change required.

docs.title: TTL hygiene
docs.text:
• Hard upper bound: CHAOS_MAX_TTL_SECONDS (default 3600).
  • The Coordinator chaos-cleanup playbook (tier low) auto-clears scenarios whose expires_at is in the past.

docs.title: Audit
docs.text:
The CRM admin router emits:

chaos.apply | scenario_id=... | target=shop|crm|both | ttl_seconds=...
chaos.clear | applied_by=<hashed-user>

Routed by the octo-chaos-audit Log Analytics parser into the Security Posture dashboard and the stale-chaos-scenario correlation rule.
